chinese: 菜肉包 Steamed Vegetable & Pork Bun

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03315595 covers steamed filled bun (baozi) as the closest class; no exact entry for vegetable-and-pork variant exists in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Steamed bun with meat and vegetable filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers steamed buns with mixed filling; no dedicated code for vegetable-pork baozi exists; closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for steamed vegetable and pork bun identified in the international release as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard steamed vegetable and pork bun weighs approximately 80 g and is typically consumed as a single-piece snack or dim sum item.

nutrient_source.title: 菜肉包 Steamed Vegetable & Pork Bun — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 菜肉包 Steamed Vegetable & Pork Bun (cr_id G00010070). All per-100g macro and micronutrient values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Carbohydrate is reported as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source; this is flagged as a data anomaly — see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 菜肉包 (steamed vegetable and pork bun) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) by analogy with steamed white-flour bao / Chinese steamed bun (GI ~65, University of Sydney GI Database). Because the HPB source records carbohydrate as 0.0 g/100g — which is a known data anomaly for this dish — the glycemic load per serving is calculated against the HPB-reported carbohydrate of 0.0 g, yielding GL = 0.0. Clinically, the actual GL is expected to be substantially higher (estimated 15–20 per 80 g bun) based on the wheat-flour bun wrapper; this discrepancy is documented in notes. The GI of 65 reflects the wheat-flour bun wrapper component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit steamed vegetable and pork buns; sodium at 1050 mg/100g (840 mg per 80 g bun) represents 42% of the 2000 mg daily sodium limit in a single item, and the negligible potassium content (47 mg/100g) provides no meaningful offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour bun wrapper with estimated medium-to-high GI (~65) and significant carbohydrate load despite HPB reporting 0 g carbohydrate
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bun with caution: the wheat-flour wrapper is a refined carbohydrate with an estimated GI of 65 and a real-world carbohydrate content of approximately 40–50 g/100g (not reflected in the HPB record); portion size should be limited to one bun and paired with low-GI foods to blunt postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100g), moderate fat (10.5 g/100g), and cholesterol (89 mg/100g) from pork filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of steamed vegetable and pork buns due to the combination of very high sodium (1050 mg/100g), moderate total fat (10.5 g/100g), and cholesterol (89 mg/100g), all of which contribute to elevated blood pressure and atherogenic risk; if consumed, limit to half a bun and choose low-sodium accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium and arachidonic acid from pork filling amplify systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise intake of steamed pork buns; sodium at 1050 mg/100g exceeds the RA threshold, and pork-derived arachidonic acid (omega-6) amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways, while the refined wheat-flour wrapper increases glycaemic load and systemic inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Vegetable and Pork Bun (菜肉包, Cài Ròu Bāo) is a classic Chinese dim sum and hawker-centre staple widely consumed in Singapore. The bun consists of a soft, fluffy steamed white-flour wrapper encasing a savoury filling of finely minced pork and chopped vegetables — typically cabbage, spring onion, and sometimes water chestnuts — seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger. The bun is prepared entirely by steaming, giving the wrapper a pillowy, slightly chewy texture and the filling a moist, tender bite with a delicate umami flavour and a mild vegetable crunch. Nutritionally, the HPB Singapore Foodo database records 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, and 10.5 g fat per 100 g, with sodium at a very high 1050 mg/100 g. The carbohydrate value of 0.0 g/100 g recorded by HPB is a known data anomaly — the wheat-flour bun wrapper typically contributes 40–50 g carbohydrate per 100 g in comparable products. Clinically, the bun is high concern for hypertension (sodium 1050 mg/100 g), Type 2 Diabetes (refined carbohydrate wrapper, estimated GI ~65), and cardiovascular disease (high sodium, moderate fat, 89 mg/100 g cholesterol). A typical serving is one bun (~80 g). The dish is not halal due to pork content and contains gluten allergens from the wheat-flour wrapper.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010070), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The carbohydrate anomaly (0.0 g/100g) is documented and does not reduce source confidence — it reflects a known HPB data limitation for this record. GI is estimated by analogy with steamed white-flour bao from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: CARBOHYDRATE ANOMALY: carbohydrate_g is reported as 0.0 g/100g by the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this record. This is inconsistent with the food's actual composition — a steamed wheat-flour bun wrapper typically contributes 40–50 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The value is preserved verbatim from the approved source (HPB) per Project Veritas source policy; however, users and clinicians should treat the carbohydrate and sugar values as a known data gap. The zero carbohydrate value is confirmed zero per the HPB source record, not naturally zero. SUGAR ANOMALY: sugar_g is 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero per HPB source, though likely understated given the wheat-flour wrapper. DIETARY FIBRE ANOMALY: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero per HPB source; likely understated. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB record are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. GLYCEMIC LOAD NOTE: GL per serving is calculated as 0.0 based on the HPB-reported carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100g; the real-world GL is estimated at 15–20 per 80 g bun based on the wheat-flour wrapper carbohydrate content. HALAL STATUS: This bun contains pork and is not halal.
